message: agent2: implementing Global Filter Registry (register_filter/unregister_filter) in outlines/prompts.py. Will add module-level registry dict and wire it into Prompt rendering (local filters take precedence). Will try to keep changes additive/isolated to minimize collision.

agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' - modifying outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str, Prompt.from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). Will ping before finalizing in case of overlap.

agent1: I'm adding create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) helper in outlines/prompts.py that centralizes env creation + built-in filter registration, used by _template_from_str/_template_from_file. I'll add a 'filters' param threaded through Prompt.from_str/from_file/prompt decorator. If you wire a global registry, consider applying it inside create_jinja_env (before local filters override) so we don't duplicate env-creation logic. Will share diff once done.

Thanks for the heads up. My task: register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) storing a module-level global filter dict, applied in env creation with LOCAL filters taking precedence over global ones. Since you're adding a create_jinja_env helper + local filters param to the decorator/from_str/from_file, could you have it accept filters as a dict and apply them like: env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY); env.filters.update(local_filters or {})? That way local > global naturally. I'll add the registry dict + register/unregister functions myself. Ping me when you land your create_jinja_env so I can hook into it without clobbering your diff.

create_jinja_env is landed in outlines/prompts.py. Signature: create_jinja_env(loader=None, filters=None) -> jinja2.Environment. Body: builds env, sets built-in filters (name/description/source/signature/schema/args), then 'if filters: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end. For your global registry, insert 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY)' right before that final local-filters update block (after built-ins, before local filters) so precedence is builtins < global registry < local filters. That's the only place you should need to touch in this function. I'm not implementing the registry myself, just leaving the hook. Let me know when your part's in so we don't stomp on each other's final diff.

My part is done and patch.txt written. Added GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY dict + register_filter(name, func)/unregister_filter(name) in outlines/prompts.py, and wired 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY)' into both _template_from_str (after built-in filters) and _template_from_file (which previously set no filters at all). Since my sandbox doesn't have your create_jinja_env refactor, I applied the registry directly at the two existing env-creation sites in the pre-refactor file. When merging: in your create_jinja_env, just add 'env.filters.update(GLOBAL_FILTER_REGISTRY)' after built-ins and before the local filters update — that reproduces the same precedence (builtins < global registry < local). Tests pass (13/13 in test_prompts.py). Good luck!
